title: Osteoarthritis
chief_complaint: My knees hurt when I walk, especially going downstairs. They're stiff in the morning but it only lasts 10 minutes. My fingers have these bumps on the joints.

physical_exam.Hands: Heberden nodes (DIP joints) and Bouchard nodes (PIP joints). No synovitis. No MCP involvement.
physical_exam.Knees: Bilateral knee crepitus. Reduced flexion. No effusion. Mild varus deformity right knee.
physical_exam.Hips: Pain with internal rotation right hip.
physical_exam.Spine: Lumbar tenderness. Reduced range of motion.

hidden_data.xray_knees: Bilateral medial compartment joint space narrowing. Subchondral sclerosis. Osteophyte formation. No erosions. Right knee: Varus deformity.
hidden_data.xray_hands: DIP and PIP joint space narrowing. Osteophytes. No erosions. No periarticular osteopenia.

key_learning_points: OA = most common joint disease. Degenerative, not inflammatory. Cartilage loss → joint space narrowing + osteophytes
OA vs RA: OA = DIP + PIP (Heberden/Bouchard nodes), no MCP, no systemic inflammation, normal ESR/CRP, non-erosive. RA = MCP + PIP, spares DIP, synovitis, elevated ESR/CRP, erosive
Morning stiffness <30 min (vs RA >1 hour). Pain worse with activity, better with rest (vs RA: better with activity)
Treatment: Exercise (strengthening, low-impact cardio), weight loss (reduces knee OA pain 30%), Acetaminophen (first-line), NSAIDs topical or oral, intra-articular steroids for flares
Surgery: Total joint replacement for refractory pain with significant functional limitation. No disease-modifying drugs exist for OA
